name: rad-website-design
description: Vision-implementer for 2026 web design — translates a user's vision into code that meets modern standards, and proposes concrete fixes for existing sites. Nine skills: knowledge layer (must-do baseline, should-do recommendations, severity-tiered anti-patterns with damage cited from real research, aesthetic direction with Calm UX default, agentic UX patterns, AEO/llms.txt with adoption caveats), action layer (implement = vision-to-code execution discipline; improve = identify issues + propose fixes for source paths or live URLs; review = pure static-analysis report). **One autonomous reviewer agent** scans HTML/CSS/JSX source. **Three pure-stdlib Python validators** (viewport-meta, pure-bw, tap-targets) run before LLM judgment. **Honest scope:** does NOT brainstorm or plan (use rad-brainstormer / rad-planner upstream), does NOT run Lighthouse, does NOT run axe-core at runtime, does NOT measure real Core Web Vitals, does NOT take screenshots, does NOT render JS-heavy SPAs. Findings tagged [STATIC] / [HEURISTIC] / [NEEDS-MANUAL]; no Pass/Fail verdict. Pair with chrome-devtools-mcp for runtime/visual layer, Lighthouse for perf measurement, rad-a11y for deeper accessibility.
